channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: PREVIEW: 1047’s EMPULSE Will Learn From Splitgate’s Mistakes
item.content:encoded:
It has been a few weeks since 1047 Games unveiled EMPULSE, an all-new first-person shooter with high-octane combat mechanics and fluid verticality. It builds on everything the company did with Splitgate, which didn’t go all that well, and attempts to innovate to the next level, producing a shooter that can stand out and make a name for itself.

During Summer Game Fest’s Play Days event, I got hands-on with EMPULSE, sitting down with 1047 to learn everything about their new shooter. In particular, I was interested in learning how the team could build on the mistakes made with Splitgate, a free-to-play live-service shooter that fell out of favor with the wider community and failed to gain any traction, despite numerous chances.

EMPULSE Doesn’t Want To Be ‘Splitgate 3’

EMPULSE is a totally separate game from Splitgate, a series that debuted in 2019 with an early access launch on PC. It was officially released in 2022, and in 2025, a follow-up title was released, with 1047 shortly bringing about the end of the first game’s operations.

However, Splitgate 2 wasn’t long for this world, and mere weeks after launch, 1047 reverted to a beta state, went silent, and relaunched the game months later, in December 2025, as Splitgate: Arena Reloaded.

That, too, failed to gain much in the way of traction or player retention.

Now, 1047 Games has emerged with EMPULSE, a high-energy first-person shooter that does away with the ‘portal’ gimmick from Splitgate and replaces it with pilotable mech suits, armed to the teeth and toting thick armor plating (and energy shields, etc.)

EMPULSE is a traditional 6v6 arena shooter with typical game modes, a perk system, a decent array of weapons and gadgets, and multiple ways to play. It uses a system called ‘P.A.I.N.T.’, which is made up of bombs that players can use to manipulate the battlefield, altering the flow of play in their favor.

It’s easy to see how this game was inspired by Titanfall, which is something 1047 Games has been transparent about. Aside from the obvious mech element, there are grapple hooks, wall running mechanics, and high-tech weapons and tools for players to lean on.

Having played EMPULSE in Los Angeles alongside the developers, including CEO Ian Proulx, I can say that it’s an interesting shooter. It has good bones, and the movement is extremely fluid and well done. The weapons feel entertaining, the base mechanics are very easy to learn, and the mech system adds a bit of variety to a traditional FPS game.

One of my biggest gripes with Splitgate was how the portal mechanics felt like a gimmick for the sake of having a gimmick, and it was clunky, complex, and too much to think about when trying to run and gun. I didn’t get that sense at all with EMPULSE, fortunately.

Do I think it’ll be a roaring success when it launches into early access on June 24th? I’ll be frank – probably not. There’s a lot of bad juju surrounding 1047 Games, thanks to layoffs, failed projects, and an insistence to keep trying to innovate while focusing on reproducing concepts from games we’ve been familiar with for years.

I do hope that the studio will find a community with EMPULSE, and that it’ll at least pull in enough players to stay afloat for some time, as the team is overdue for a win.

When I was talking to the developers at Play Days, I asked them directly how Splitgate’s mistakes have fuelled their desire to succeed with EMPULSE, and they were refreshingly transparent.

This is all about forging a game that is built with the community in mind. Gone are the plans for a free-to-play, heavily monetized shooter, and what remains is a multiplayer title with a budget entry point and no microtransactions in early access. It’ll cost just $19.99 to secure your copy of EMPULSE, and that’s all you realistically ever need to pay.

I also learned that a competitive ecosystem will be fostered, but that things aren’t expected just to grow overnight. The team is critically aware that an organic community must form around EMPULSE for it to succeed, and they’re more than open to feeding the esports-focused side of things, as long as the players want that.

It helps that, earlier this year, 1047 Games hired a veteran of Rocket League, who comes aboard with stacks of competitive gaming experience.

It’s not clear when microtransactions will surface in EMPULSE, as it’s inevitable that they will, but it’s a testament to 1047’s attempt to regain some lost trust in players by launching into early access without even having an in-game store.

EMPULSE enters early access on June 24th on PC, PS5, and XBOX. Are you interested in securing a copy? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that 007: First Light could be getting a sequel

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: NYT Connections Hints Today (#1094)—Answers for Tuesday, June 9
item.content:encoded:
It’s time to add another notch onto that NYT Connections streak. In this guide, you’ll find the NYT Connections hints and solutions you need to power through the puzzle and come out the other side without too much fuss. If you’re stuck on today’s NYT Connections challenge, simply refer to the hints below!

Scroll down to get the NYT Connections answers you need!

How to Play NYT Connections

In June 2023, NYT Connections emerged as yet another game in the ever-growing lineup of the New York Times titles. This title soared immediately, and it’s easy to see why. As a free-to-play, accessible New York Times game, NYT Connections is available worldwide and can be played on desktop devices or in a mobile app.

But how do you play NYT Connections?

The NYT team will provide you with a grid of 16 words once a day. The only task left up to you is to sort those words into four groups of four by finding their commonalities. Every NYT Connections puzzle has just one solution, and some words can have double meanings, so it can sometimes be tricky to figure out the answers.

It’s also important to stress that the words are color-coded: yellow, blue, green, and purple. That’s in order of difficulty, from least to most.

What’s critical here is that you get four guesses every day, and if you use those, your NYT Connections answers for today will be out of reach, as it’ll be game over.

Today’s NYT Connections Hints (June 9)

Here are the words for today’s NYT Connections:

  • ASTERISK
  • AXE
  • ANGEL
  • SURPRISE
  • SECRET
  • TRADEMARK
  • BONE
  • DOVE
  • DEGREE
  • PASSWORD
  • LAMB
  • KEYS
  • BABE
  • SKINS
  • EXPONENT
  • SPOILER

And here are the hints I can offer for today’s NYT Connections:

  • Yellow – Things that could symbolize innocence
  • Green – Words and things you’re not supposed to reveal
  • Blue – Elements you might see in superscript in a document somewhere
  • Purple – Slang terms for musical instruments

Here’s the final warning before we get to the NYT Connections answers for today, June 9!

Are you sure you want to scroll further?

Very well!

NYT Connections Answers Explained for Today, June 9 (#1094)

If you want to jump straight to the answers for today’s NYT Connections puzzle, I’ve got you covered:

  • Yellow – ANGEL, BABE, DOVE, LAMB
  • Green – PASSWORD, SECRET, SPOILER, SURPRISE
  • Blue – ASTERISK, DEGREE, EXPONENT, TRADEMARK
  • Purple – AXE, BONE, KEYS, SKINS

When Does NYT Connections Reset?

That’s today’s puzzle, but if you want the next one, the game resets daily at midnight local time (that’s your local time). You’ll have 24 hours to complete the puzzle when midnight ticks over in your timezone.

How did you do? Did you get a perfect run? Let me know what your streak looks like on the Insider Gaming Discord server

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

item.title: Persona 6 Could Be Scheduled For 2027, It’s Claimed
item.content:encoded:
Persona 6 was finally unveiled during the XBOX Games Showcase, though what we saw was only a short teaser for the upcoming sequel. It was enough to get fans excited for the future of the franchise, but little else was shared regarding gameplay or its release window.

That said, a reliable leaker for Atlus and SEGA-related content has suggested that, despite the lack of a launch window in the trailer, Persona 6 could very much be on track to release in 2027, even though Persona 4 Revival is also scheduled for the same year.

2027 Could Be The Year Of Persona

The rumors come from user lolilolailo on resetera, who shared in a post:

Don’t read too much on the teaser not having 2027 in it, especially considering P4R is launching before.

Persona 4 Revival is set to launch on February 18, 2027, and with the lack of a release window in the Persona 6 trailer, it led many fans to suspect that Persona 4 Revival will be the focus of 2027, with Persona 6 still a ways away.

Of course, judging by lolilolailo’s claim, that could very well not be the case, and we could instead see another year like 2024, where Atlus released Persona 3 Reload in February, and Metaphor: ReFantazio in October. Could the same pattern be followed, with Persona 4 Revival in February, and Persona 6 late 2027?

Prior Persona 6 leaks would certainly align with this theory. SEGA issued DMCA takedowns to numerous Persona 6 leaks ahead of the big reveal, with the logo and other images spilling onto the internet.

Alongside those, a September 2027 release window was revealed, and with the leaked logo now confirmed to be correct, there are reasonable expectations that Persona 6 could very well be 2027-bound. Perhaps the lack of a date was to give Persona 4 Revival its time in the spotlight, as Persona 6 is bound to steal the show eventually.

Are you hyped for Persona 6? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more gaming news, read about whether a sequel to 007 First Light is in the works, as well as the upcoming Nintendo Direct.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

item.title: Amazon’s Head Of Gaming Thinks 007 First Light Sequel ‘Should Be Made’
item.content:encoded:
Over the past week or so, there’s been a lot of discussion surrounding the future of James Bond following the breakout success of 007 First Light. It’s sold three million copies in under two weeks, and fans are already desperate to know if a sequel is on the way.

Well, just a few days back, it seemed like it was ‘too early’ for Amazon to discuss Bond’s future, with suggestions that future titles will be handled by Amazon and MGM instead of IO Interactive. As you probably know, though, things change fairly quickly in the gaming industry, and Amazon’s Head of Gaming has now revealed that a sequel to 007 First Light ‘should be made.’

007’s Future Looks Bright

Speaking with IGN at Summer Game Fest, Jeff Gattis, Amazon’s Head of Gaming, discussed what the future of 007 looks like now that First Light has been so successful.

Firstly, he clarified Amazon’s position on the matter, noting that he ‘did not confirm Amazon Game Studios is publishing the next one.’ He continued to heap praise on IO Interactive, revealing that they’ve been ‘great partners’ to work with.

Now it’s onto the juicy bit, as Gattis began to delve deeper into the future of the franchise. As was already suggested before, he reiterated that ‘as long as we feel there’s a desire for players to have the game, it’s safe to say it’s going to be made.’

Well, with three million copies sold, the likelihood of a healthy profit, and heaps of critical praise, it seems like there’s more than enough demand for a sequel, leading Gattis to close with:

Seems like a sequel should be made.

Hopefully, that should put to bed the concern that the future of Bond is up in the air, and we can enjoy 007 First Light with the hope that a sequel is just a matter of time away.

Have you played 007 First Light? Would you be hoping for a sequel? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more gaming news, read about how XBOX will decide on its console exclusives, as well as the upcoming Nintendo Direct.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

item.title: NYT Strands #828 Hints and Answers for Today, June 9, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Strands is a daily word search puzzle game from The New York Times. It is a relatively new game, launched in 2024, in which players seek a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid.

Whether it’s your first time playing or you’re a Strands veteran, today’s tips and strategies can help you find words faster. So, if you’ve found yourself stuck with today’s Strands, don’t worry; below you’ll find some helpful hints and even the answers for Strands Game #828 for Tuesday, June 9, 2026

To play today’s Strands, head to the game’s website.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s edition of Strands. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How To Play Strands

Strands is a daily word search that challenges you to find words with a common theme. There’s also a Spanagram that reveals the deeper theme behind the words.

  • Words that are three letters or fewer do not count.
  • Words that reveal the correct theme appear in blue.
  • The Spanagram turns yellow when found.
  • Finding three words that don’t fit the theme will reveal a hint to help you find the proper words.

The goal is to string together as many words as possible. Every letter on the grid gets used! Daily puzzles keep players coming back for more, making it a fun brain exercise and a popular online trend.

Strands can only be played on the New York Times website or on the NYT Games app.

Strands Hints for Today, June 9, 2026

The Strands’ answers to the theme of Game #828 are all terms related to boating.

General Strands Hints for June 9

Without giving today’s Strands answers completely away, here are a few helpful hints for today to get you started:

  • Hint 1: If you’re a surfer, you’re always looking for the next big one.
  • Hint 2: Patrick Swayze and John Travolta were big hits in movies that involved this.
  • Hint 3: The spangram was a famous track by The Lonely Island.

Use these clues to narrow down your searches and increase your chances of solving Strands #828 for June 9, 2026.

Words to Help You Find Strand Hints for June 9, 2026

If you want help getting started without having today’s Strands answer given to you, there are a few starter words worth trying that will help you unlock some in-game hints. A few starter words that may help are:

  • SING
  • BANS
  • DATE
  • DATES
  • SACK

Today’s Strands Answer for Game #828 on June 9, 2026

If you’ve tried everything, including using the above hints, and are still stuck, you can find the answers to today’s Strands below. Check out the answers for past puzzles here.

  • WAVES
  • SNACKS
  • DRINKS
  • DANCING
  • SEASICKNESS
  • DECK
  • SPANAGRAM: IMONABOAT

If you solved today’s Strands without our answers, congratulations! If not, take note of the best tips above for tomorrow’s puzzle, and check back daily for more hints.

When Is The Next Strands Game After #828?

All New York Times games are updated daily at 12 am or Midnight in your respective time zone. Information about the next game is often available in the hours leading up to it, giving players a heads-up. The game on June 10 will be Strands Game #829.

What Is Strands?

Strands is a daily word search puzzle game created by The New York Times. Launched in 2024, Strands challenges players to discover a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid. The game’s original concept was developed by Juliette Seive, a research director on The New York Times Games team, with the aim of replicating Wordle’s success. Tracy Bennett has served as the puzzle editor for Strands, overseeing the creation and curation of daily puzzles since the publication’s launch.

For even more Insider Gaming delivered straight to your inbox, sign up for our weekly newsletter.
